title: mikania palustris
intro: Mikania palustris, also known as swamp crinum or wild hops, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its yellowish flowers and distinctive, pungent odor. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mikania palustris include relief from arthritis, muscle spasms, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Mikania palustris include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves or roots in hot water.
